pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'COMPUTE LAYER: ... An Auto Scaling Group encompassing the EC2 instances.' Lines 5 of task.json and setup_task.sh requirements document item '1 x Auto Scaling Group (ASG)' confirm ASG is required but it is missing from metadata list.

README.md success criteria table and verifier.py line 48 confirm minimum of 15 shapes required for full points. Task description mentions: VPC, 4 subnets, IGW, 2 NAT gateways, ALB, 2 EC2 instances, ASG, RDS, ElastiCache, S3, CloudFront, Route 53, plus 3 security zone rectangles = ~20 minimum shapes, so 15 is a reasonable threshold.

README.md success criteria table and verifier.py line 49 confirm minimum of 8 connection edges. Task description requires connections between: ALB-EC2, EC2-RDS, EC2-ElastiCache, CloudFront-ALB, Route53-CloudFront, IGW-ALB, and subnet interconnections. 8 edges is achievable and verified.

Task description line 5 explicitly states: 'Create a second page titled Data Flow' and README.md section 'DIAGRAM REQUIREMENTS' specifies 'Page 1 — Architecture Overview' and 'Page 2 — Data Flow'. Verifier.py line 146 checks for >= 2 pages.

Task description says 'Save as ~/Desktop/aws_architecture.drawio'. User is 'ga' (from login: username=ga). Expanded path is /home/ga/Desktop/aws_architecture.drawio. Verified in setup_task.sh line 22 and verifier.py line 53.

Task description says 'export page 1 as ~/Desktop/aws_architecture.png'. User is 'ga'. Expanded path is /home/ga/Desktop/aws_architecture.png. Verified in setup_task.sh line 23 and verifier.py export_result.sh line 9.

pi_items.metadata_value: VPC, subnet, EC2, RDS PostgreSQL, ElastiCache Redis, S3, ALB, CloudFront, Route 53, NAT Gateway, Internet Gateway, Auto Scaling Group, WAF, Certificate Manager, CloudWatch
pi_items.verified_value: All mentioned services are real AWS services verified via AWS documentation and AWS Well-Architected Framework
pi_items.source: Cross-referenced with AWS official documentation at docs.aws.amazon.com. All services are real and documented as part of multi-tenant SaaS architecture patterns.

pi_items.metadata_value: Based on AWS Well-Architected Framework (SaaS Lens)
pi_items.verified_value: AWS Well-Architected Framework SaaS Lens exists and documents multi-tenant SaaS patterns. AWS Prescriptive Guidance provides specific PostgreSQL multi-tenant implementation patterns.
pi_items.source: AWS official documentation: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/wellarchitected/latest/saas-lens/welcome.html and https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/saas-multitenant-managed-postgresql/welcome.html confirm the reference architecture basis is real and documented.

pi_items.verified_value: PostgreSQL 14 is a valid RDS engine option. Released October 2021. Current as of 2026 for multi-tenant SaaS applications.
pi_items.source: AWS RDS documentation confirms PostgreSQL 14 support and AWS prescriptive guidance for multi-tenant SaaS with PostgreSQL confirms version 14 is current.

pi_items.metadata_value: ElastiCache Redis cluster paired with RDS PostgreSQL is valid architecture
pi_items.verified_value: ElastiCache Redis is documented as a caching layer for RDS PostgreSQL. AWS prescriptive guidance confirms this combination for multi-tenant SaaS.
pi_items.source: AWS blogs and documentation at aws.amazon.com/blogs/database/ confirm ElastiCache Redis with RDS PostgreSQL is a standard multi-AZ, high-availability pattern with 55% cost savings and up to 80x faster read performance.

pi_items.metadata_value: 2 Availability Zones (us-east-1a, us-east-1b) with matching public and private subnets
pi_items.verified_value: Multi-AZ deployment across 2 availability zones is a standard AWS architecture pattern documented in Well-Architected Framework for high availability.
pi_items.source: Setup_task.sh requirements document specifies us-east-1 region (real AWS region) with 2 AZs and 4 subnets (2 public, 2 private). This matches AWS best practices.

privileged_info_summary: The aws_saas_architecture task requires agents to create a multi-page AWS architecture diagram using draw.io. The task is based on real AWS services and the verified AWS Well-Architected Framework SaaS Lens documentation. Required components include VPC, 4 subnets across 2 availability zones, EC2 instances, RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis, S3, ALB, CloudFront, Route 53, NAT Gateways, Internet Gateway, and Auto Scaling Groups. The diagram requires minimum 15 shapes and 8 connection edges. Two pages are required: an Architecture Overview page and a Data Flow sequence page. Output files are aws_architecture.drawio (draw.io format) and aws_architecture.png (PNG export). One discrepancy noted: the task description explicitly requires Auto Scaling Group but it is missing from the metadata's required_components list.
